ford_ranger_job_note_35cf72aa67.text: Silca explicitly lists Ranger P375 from 2015 with HU101, 433 MHz and ID47. This provides a locksmith-relevant security breakpoint from the earlier DST80 application.

ford_ranger_job_note_b9e9278ea7.text: Transpondery confirms Texas 4C for 1999-2000. The early UK Ranger is Mazda-derived, so the blade and remote must not be inferred from later HU101 Ford applications.

ford_ranger_job_note_adc13f444f.text: Transpondery confirms Texas 4C for 1999-2000. The early UK Ranger is Mazda-derived, so the blade and remote must not be inferred from later HU101 Ford applications. Some key hardware varies within this year/build range. Confirm the original key, VIN and physical vehicle configuration before supply.

ford_ranger_job_note_db5af4f282.text: Transpondery confirms Texas 4D63 for 2001-2010. Because this is a Mazda-derived UK platform, blade and frequency remain tied to the exact application rather than later Ford T6 data.

ford_ranger_job_note_06e0fdd944.text: Transpondery confirms Texas 4D63 for 2001-2010. Because this is a Mazda-derived UK platform, blade and frequency remain tied to the exact application rather than later Ford T6 data. Some key hardware varies within this year/build range. Confirm the original key, VIN and physical vehicle configuration before supply.

ford_ranger_job_note_546d0ced8a.text: Transpondery lists Ranger 2011+ as DST80. Silca independently lists Ranger TKE 2011-2015 with HU101 and 433 MHz; this record stops before the separately catalogued 2015 Hitag Pro application.

ford_ranger_job_note_f835e9085d.text: Use only where passive start is physically confirmed. Blade, frequency and Hitag Pro family are supported by the 2015-on Silca Ranger application.

verification.notes: Duplicate wording is collapsed in this index. Each note remains traceable to the verified generation-specific records in models.json.
